title: ZLAR Receipt v1 Payload
description: The governance fields inside a v1 receipt envelope. This JSON is canonicalized per ZLAR Canonicalization Spec v1.0, then base64url-encoded into the envelope's 'payload' field.

tool.description: Tool name as received by the gate (e.g., Bash, Edit, Write).

domain.description: Policy domain classification (e.g., file, network, bash, mcp).

detail_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical detail object. Proves what action was governed without leaking specifics.

ts.description: Decision timestamp. ISO 8601 UTC, always 3 decimal places (e.g., 2026-04-06T14:00:00.000Z).

policy_version.description: Version of the policy that was evaluated.

manifest_agent_id.description: Agent identity from the manifest, or null. Declared identity: what the agent claims to be under its signed manifest.

manifest_principal.description: Human principal accountable for the agent, or null. Declared identity: from the signed manifest.

agent_config_hash.description: SHA-256 (hex, lowercase) of the primary governing configuration artifact actually in effect at decision time. Hashes the raw file bytes as loaded by the gate — no line-ending normalization, no encoding conversion. Null when no governing artifact is present in the resolution set. Proves the identity of the primary governing artifact, not the full transitive instruction/context surface. Resolution order (first match wins): project CLAUDE.md, user CLAUDE.md (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md), project soul.md, project should.md.

agent_config_source.description: Which precedence tier produced agent_config_hash. Null iff agent_config_hash is null. Enables verifiers to distinguish same-content hashes across different source tiers.

agent_fingerprint.description: Short identity handle — first 16 hex chars of SHA-256('agent_type:agent_config_hash:policy_version'). Convenience index for correlation across receipts; not normative evidence. Null when agent_config_hash is null.

delegation_chain.description: Delegation chain. Empty array if no delegation.

audit_event_id.description: ID of the source audit trail entry.

audit_prev_hash.description: prev_hash from the audit entry. Anchors receipt to the hash chain.

escalation_source.description: Escalation routing source for this action (e.g., restore, canary). Null when not applicable.

h15_elapsed_seconds.description: H15 audit field (canary v1 Element D). Actual response time in seconds, integer (ZLAR canonicalization forbids floats — see lib/canonicalize.mjs). Sub-second elapsed times round/truncate to 0; this is acceptable because (a) H14 variance threshold is 4s, much larger than 1s noise, and (b) sub-second elapsed is the canary signal regardless of exact value. Null when not measured (e.g., gate-side decisions, errors before timing capture).

h15_floor_seconds.description: H15 audit field (canary v1 Element D). The H15 response-time floor that would have applied to this severity, integer seconds (ZLAR canonical form forbids floats). Floor values today are whole-second (3/10/30) so no precision loss. Null when no floor applies.

h15_below_floor.description: H15 audit field (canary v1 Element D). True iff h15_elapsed_seconds < h15_floor_seconds. Audit-significant for warn/info severities once Element A ships canary-zone semantics; for critical severities a true value should not occur in normal flow. Null when either elapsed or floor is null. Optional for backward compatibility.

h14_alert_tier.description: H14 audit field (canary v1 Element D). Canary alert tier emitted alongside this decision. 0 = no alert (default), 1 = banner, 2 = pre-confirm, 3 = forced pause. Tiers 1+ require Elements B+E behavior to be live; emission emits 0 in this slice. See doctrine/roadmap/human-attention-canary.md.

h14_alert_ack_receipt_id.description: H14 audit field (canary v1 Element D). Receipt ID of a prior canary alert acknowledgement that preceded this decision, if any. Null when no canary ack flow occurred. Format matches the envelope id pattern. Optional for backward compatibility.
